creator: jrhett@svcolo.com
text:
On Jan 9, 2008, at 10:53 AM, Eygene Ryabinkin wrote:
>>> The CFEngine manual at
>>> http://www.cfengine.org/docs/cfengine-Reference.html
>>
>> is very often out of date ;-)  Best to submit patches for that.
>
> May be.  But why are you telling that BDB >= 4.4 is needed?
> Do you have some negative experience?

Well the configure script says explicitly
   AC_MSG_ERROR(This release of cfengine requires a version of  
BerkeleyDB 4.4 or later)

>>> Ah, that's the crucial part.  If true, then it's patched at the  
>>> source.
>
> Yes, it was patched.  And this was mentioned in the follow-up to  
> the PR.=

I have the lastest trunk, and I don't see this.  The configure output  
from above was directly from the latest svn.

> I am submitting patches both to the current port and to the maintainer
> of the mainline code.  And I am trying to push both of them, because
> the port for the next version can be delayed (or the next version
> can be delayed by itself) and I prefer the ports to make their job.

I disagree.  And this has been debated extensively on the ports list,  
and consensus was that freebsd should not try to interfere with the  
maintainer.    Also note that both 2.2.2 and 2.2.3 have come out  
since you reported this, so frequency of updates to the mainline code  
base is not a problem.

> it.  I had sent another patch to Mark some months ago and he promised
> to analyze it once he will have time.  I will check for the state of
> that patch and if it still not brought to the mainline code, I will
> sent it to you, if you will be so kind to review it and push to the
> sources.

You need to submit to bug-cfengine@cfengine.org, which is where  
patches are dealt with.

--
Jo Rhett
Bitch and Wine.net - nothing like cool glass of wine and a warm dog
